text:
each child respectively attaining the age of 18 years or ceasing their full-time [secondary] / [tertiary] education [to first degree level] [[including] / [excluding] a gap year], whichever shall be the later; or

a further order.

[(For avoidance of doubt, where the relevant child takes a pre-tertiary education gap year, delaying the commencement of tertiary education by one year, the child periodical payments shall be suspended for that year and shall automatically revive when the child commences tertiary education.)]

The court may (prior to the expiry of the term or subsequently) order a longer period of payment.

Delayed Commencement Orders

The order in paragraph [para number] above shall only start to have effect when the CMS ceases to have jurisdiction for the child[ren] of the family because:

[he] / [she] / [they] [is] / [are] no longer in full-time, non-advanced education as set out in s55 Child Support Act 1991; or

[he ] / [she] / [they] or either party are no longer habitually resident within the jurisdiction of England and Wales.

Global order

(either)

The [applicant] / [respondent] shall pay to the [respondent] / [applicant] maintenance pending suit until the date of [decree absolute] / [the final [divorce] / [dissolution] order] and afterwards periodical payments for the benefit of [herself] / [himself] and the children of the family. Payments shall be at the rate of £[amount] per annum less any payments made by the [applicant] / [respondent] to the CMS pursuant to a CMS calculation. Payments shall start on [date], and shall end on the first to occur of:

the death of either the applicant or the respondent;

the [respondent’s] / [applicant’s] remarriage; or

a further order.

(or)
